perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained, in this agreement the parties agree that the information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its professional advisors, agents and consultants, for the purposes of evaluating the investigation and enter into an agreement as contemplated in clause 1 (introduction), providing that the receiving party takes whatever steps are necessary to procure that such professional advisors, agents and consultants agree to abide by the terms of th is agreement to prevent the unauthorised disclosure of the information to third parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained, in this agreement the parties agree that the information may be disclosed by the receiving party to its professional advisors, agents and consultants.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the section 'for the purposes of evaluating the investigation and enter into an agreement as contemplated in clause 1 (introduction), providing that the receiving party takes whatever steps are necessary to procure that such professional advisors, agents and consultants agree to abide by the terms of th is agreement to prevent the unauthorised disclosure of the information to third parties.' This means the receiving party can disclose the information to anyone without having to bound them to the agreement, contradicting clause 3.3 where parties cannot disclose to a third party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that they will not, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed disclosure to advisors, agents, and consultants for specific purposes and under the condition that they agree to abide by the agreement. The changed text removes these limitations, allowing disclosure to anyone without requiring them to be bound by the agreement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which prohibits disclosure to any third party without prior written consent, except as allowed by the agreement. The change introduces a clear contradiction because the receiving party can now disclose the information to third parties without restriction, violating the explicitly stated prohibition in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party agrees: 3.5.1 not to utilise, employ, exploit or in any other manner whatsoever use the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreem ent for any purpose without the prior express written consent of the disclosing party. 3.5.2 that the unauthorised disclosure of the information to a third party may cause irreparable loss, harm and damage to the disclosing party. Accordingly, the receiving par ty indemnifies and holds the disclosing party harmless against any loss, action, claim, harm or damage, of whatever nature, suffered by the disclosing party pursuant to a breach by the receiving party of the provisions of this agreement,
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party agrees: 3.5.2 that the unauthorised disclosure of the information to a third party may cause irreparable loss, harm and damage to the disclosing party. Accordingly, the receiving par ty indemnifies and holds the disclosing party harmless against any loss, action, claim, harm or damage, of whatever nature, suffered by the disclosing party pursuant to a breach by the receiving party of the provisions of this agreement,
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause 3.5.1 which restricts the use of information without written consent, it introduces an in text contradiction with clause 5.2. Clause 5.2 limits the information use to that 'for which it is disclosed; and in accordance with the provisions of this agreement' and the omission gives the receiving party unrestricted use of the information for any purpose regardless of written permission. Thus, if receiving party exploits it for other reason's there is no legal ground.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party undertakes not to use the information fo r purpose other than: 5.2.1 that for which it is disclosed; and 5.2.2 in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains clause 3.5.1, restricting information use without written consent. The changed text omits this clause, granting the receiving party unrestricted use. This directly contradicts clause 5.2.1 which states that the receiving party undertakes not to use the information for purpose other than that for which it is disclosed, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the receiving party can now use the information for ANY purpose even if it wasn't what it was disclosed for, therefore violating clause 5.2.1.

perturbation.original_text: All inform ation disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party is acknowledged by the receiving party: 4.1 to be proprietary to the disclosing party; and 4 4.2 not to confer any rights of whatever nature in such information to the receiving party.
perturbation.changed_text: All inform ation disclosed by the disclosing party to the receiving party is acknowledged by the receiving party: 4.1 to be proprietary to the disclosing party;
perturbation.explanation: By removing 4.2, it means the receiving party now has rights over the information, which contradicts 5.1. The receiving party can't disclose information to anyone other than its officers, employees, and professional advisors on a need-to-know basis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party may disclose the information only to its officers, employees and professional advisors and then only on a strictly need -to-know basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the receiving party acknowledges that the disclosed information is proprietary and does not confer any rights to the receiving party (4.2). The changed text removes 4.2, implying the receiving party might now have rights. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which restricts the receiving party's ability to disclose the information only to specific individuals on a need-to-know basis, thereby limiting their rights. If the receiving party now has rights due to the change, it weakens the restricitions given in the contradicted text, therefore 'YES'

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using the s ame standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that they shall protect the information disclosed pursuant to the provisions of this agreement using the s ame standard of care that each party applies to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and that the information shall be stored and handled in such a way as to prevent any unauthorised disclosure thereof.', there is no standard or requirement that the information needs to be stored securely. This creates a conflict with clause 3.3, which states that parties can't disclose the information. Without secure storage, accidental disclosure is highly probably, creating an in text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that they will not, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text ensures both protection and secure storage of information. The changed text removes the requirement for secure storage, creating a vulnerability. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits disclosure to third parties. Without secure storage, accidental or unauthorized disclosure becomes more likely, making it impossible to guarantee compliance with the non-disclosure agreement. The omission directly undermines the intent and enforceability of the non-disclosure clause.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof and it shall continue to bind the parti es for 3 (three) years after its termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be deemed to have commenced upon the date of last 6 signing hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and it shall continue to bind the parti es for 3 (three) years after its termination', it removes the enforceability of the agreement for 3 years post termination. This omission creates an in text contradiction with clause 3.3 which states that the parties will not disclose the information 'thereafter'. Now there is no agreement stating 'thereafter'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that they will not, during the course of their association with one another or, thereafter, disclose the information to any third party for any reason or purpose whatsoever without the prior written consent of the disclosing party, save in accordance with the provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a duration of enforceability for 3 years after termination. The changed text removes this duration. The contradicted text specifies obligations 'thereafter'. Without a defined period of enforceability extending 'thereafter', the obligation to not disclose information after the termination of the agreement becomes unenforceable, creating a direct contradiction. The change is an Omission that creates an In-Text Contradiction as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, specifically definition 2.
